description: The baseline classifier is evaluated, without retraining, on a test set where all 600 AI-generated texts have been paraphrased via back-translation through English. Human texts (200) remain unchanged.
key_findings: AI accuracy drops by 64 percentage points (from ~0.99 to 0.35) — below chance (0.50).
Human accuracy remains at 0.93 (unchanged), confirming the model learned LLM stylistic signatures rather than abstract 'AI-ness'.
Drop is uniform across all three generators (0.335–0.360), so brittleness is independent of which generator produced the text.

Comparable English work (Krishna et al., 2023, DIPPER attack on RoBERTa-Large-Detector) reported a 20-point drop. The Persian classifier here drops roughly 3x more, partly because parsinlu paraphrases are more aggressive than DIPPER outputs.
